articles.name: Parent Technical Support Index
articles.title: Parent Technical Support Index
articles.body:
The FCA Sports management tool, Sports Connect, maintains helpful instructions for all league and club management tasks in their help center. Below is a short index of topics that cover common tasks for parents whose children are involved in an FCA Sports program:

  • Getting Started (i.e., Registration, refunds, cancellations, etc.)
  • FAQs
  • Navigating my Account

FYI

All Sports Connect related questions can also be directed to the Sports Connect help desk.

Or call 1-866-258-3303.

articles.name: Introduction
articles.title: Introduction
articles.body:
In FCA Sports, we understand the profound impact youth sports can have on a child’s life. Simply put, we want to see lives transformed by an encounter with Jesus Christ through the vehicle of youth sports. As we understand who we are and why we exist as FCA Sports, it allows us to effectively live out our values and communicate to athletes and parents we hope will join our leagues and clubs.

What FCA Sports Is All About

FCA Sports provides exceptional youth sports experiences shaped by coaches and volunteers committed to helping athletes not only sharpen their skills but discover truth, belonging, and strength in something greater than themselves.

Why FCA Sports Exists and What We Do

We live in a world of constant comparison. Every day, we are bombarded by messages telling us we are not good enough, and as a result, too many young people are confused about their worth. Like you, we believe every young athlete is amazing in their own unique way. Every young athlete deserves to believe that about themselves, too.

Youth sports have never been just a game. From the moment we step into the game, sports begin training us for life. We learn perseverance in difficult times, humility through defeat, the joy of victory. We build camaraderie with teammates, respect our opponents, and look up to our coaches. Playing sports teaches us character, and when combined with the inner strength and self-worth we gain in the process, the transformation lasts forever.

FCA Sports exists to support coaches committed to helping young athletes develop an unwavering belief in their individual worth as they sharpen their skills and have fun playing the game. We nurture that self-confidence by creating an uncommon environment where we invest in each player individually, developing their athletic ability, instilling profound truth, and fostering connection so they can boldly give their all in everything they do.

How FCA Sports Is Different than Other Clubs and Leagues

FCA Sports coaches desire more than just developing an athlete’s skills on the field of play, they want to develop character and encourage spiritual growth. For these coaches, their time is an opportunity to minister to athletes through sports. FCA Sports will compete with excellence but will always be focused on the relationship between the athlete and the coach as primary. Ministering to the hearts of players and helping them grow in their faith is why FCA Sports exists. We will compete hard, but we will focus on more than wins and losses. We seek to develop players mentally, physically, and spiritually, helping them grow into complete athletes.

articles.name: Defining Leagues and Clubs
articles.title: Defining Leagues and Clubs
articles.body:
League

The typical league is a self-contained experience where teams play each other within the same league. No “travel” is involved. Evaluations are held, but all players make the team. The season lasts about ten weeks and the cost is around $100 for the season. A league is ideal for both an introductory sports experience (novice) and a competent skill set as athletes age up through the league.

Club

The typical club is an aspirational experience where athletes invest in multiple practices per week and travel to play teams from other clubs in tournaments. Try-outs are held and only the top athletes make the team. A club team experience can last up to 9-months with a cost between $1,500 to $4,000 for the season/year. A club is ideal for athletes committed to advancing in the sport to an expert level.

articles.name: Reporting Abuse or Misconduct (Parents or Athletes)
articles.title: Reporting Abuse or Misconduct (Parents or Athletes)
articles.body:
All FCA Sports representatives, from the program Director to the coaches and volunteers, will be SafeSport trained and certified prior to working with the athletes in the FCA Sports program. Program Directors will also be trained in FCA Sports Youth Protection Policies.

Their status as FCA Sports representatives also obligate them as mandatory reporters of abuse.

However, parents, as well as athletes, can also report abuse or misconduct that may occur within an FCA Sports environment through Player's Health Protect. The FCA Sports Player's Health Protect Website can be accessed from your program's Sports Connect page. It may be found in the menu on the site's home page, like in the image below:

You can also access the reporting site directly at the link below.

Player's Health Protect Reporting Website

For more information on how to report abuse or misconduct at the FCA Sports Player's Health Protect Website, refer to the "Reporting an Incident in Player's Health Protect" article.

articles.name: Language Quick Guide
articles.title: Language Quick Guide

articles.name: Reporting an Incident in Player's Health Protect
articles.title: Reporting an Incident in Player's Health Protect
articles.body:
All FCA Sports representatives, from the program Director to the coaches and even the volunteers, are considered mandatory reporters of abuse. Mandatory reporters are obligated to report any signs of abuse they observe.

In general, there are 2 basics kinds of abuse that may occur:

  1. Abuse occurring within an FCA Sports environment (such as hazing, bullying by one athlete to another or by a coach toward an athlete).
  2. Abuse occurring outside the FCA Sports environment (such as neglect or sexual abuse by a parent or guardian of an athlete participating in FCA Sports).

Abuse occurring outside of an FCA Sports environment must be reported by FCA Sports representatives when it is observed. These forms of abuse can be reported directly to local authorities. Refer to the "State-by-State Numbers for Reporting Abuse" to report to the proper authorities.

Attention

Contact local authorities to report an incident immediately. Then notify your FCA Sports program director.

To report abuse that may occur within an FCA Sports program, mandatory reports must do so through Player's Health Protect. A report can be filed from the Player's Health Protect Reporting Website or through the Player's Health Protect mobile app.

Player's Health Protect Reporting Website

FYI

The FCA Sports Player's Health Protect Website will be accessible from every FCA Sports program site (provided by Sports Connect).

To report an incident from the FCA Sports Player's Health Protect Website, click the chat widget in the bottom right-hand corner:

You can also report an incident through the Player's Health Protect mobile app which is available for Apple and Android.

Attention

All FCA Sports representatives must download the Player's Health Protect mobile app and log in using their Player's Health Protect credentials.

articles.name: Ministry Leader Application (MLA)
articles.title: Ministry Leader Application (MLA)
articles.body:
Every coach of an FCA Sports program must complete a screening process before they can begin coaching. That screening process involves:

  1. Completing a Ministry Leader Application (MLA) with FCA.
  2. Completing a background check.
  3. Completing the 90-minute SafeSport Training (and gaining SafeSport Certification).

To begin the process, your FCA Sports program Director will register you for the program within Sports Connect (the FCA Sports program management system). You will receive a confirmation email from Sports Connect with, among other things, a link to the "Ministry Leader Application (MLA)." The same link can be found below:

Ministry Leader Application

While completing the MLA, select the appropriate role under "Step 3: Opportunities":

FYI

The MLA includes questions regarding your agreement with FCA’s Statement of Faith, your personal testimony and your commitment to daily Christian living as well as adherence to all Youth Protection Guidelines and policies.

You cannot continue as an FCA Sports coach without completing the MLA!

After submitting your MLA, it must be approved by an FCA staff person.

articles.name: Statement of Faith
articles.title: Statement of Faith
articles.body:
We believe the Bible to be the only inspired, trustworthy, and true, without error, Word of God.

(2 Timothy 3:16-17)

We believe there is only one God who eternally exists in three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

(Matthew 28:19)

We believe Jesus Christ is God, in His virgin birth, in His sinless life, in His miracles, in His death that paid for our sin through His shed blood, in His bodily resurrection, in His ascension/rising up to the right hand of the Father, and in His personal return in power and glory.

(John 1:1; Matthew 1:18,25; Hebrews 4:15; Hebrews 9:15-22; 1 Corinthians 15:1-8; Acts 1:9-11; Hebrews 9:27-28)

We believe that acceptance of Jesus Christ and the corresponding renewal of the Holy Spirit are the only paths to salvation for lost/sinful men and women.

(John 3:16; John 5:24; Titus 3:3-7)

We believe in the present ministry of the Holy Spirit, who lives within and guides Christians so they are enabled to live godly lives.

(John 14:15-26; John 16:5-16; Ephesians 1:13-14)

We believe in eternal life, and that through belief in Jesus Christ as the Son of God, we spend eternity with the Lord in Heaven. We believe that in rejecting Jesus Christ as Lord and Savior, we receive eternal suffering in hell.

(Matthew 25:31-46; 1 Thessalonians 4:13-18)

We believe in the spiritual unity of believers in our Lord Jesus Christ, that all believers are members of His body, the Church.

(Philippians 2:1-4)
